text: The video shows previous surgical incisions located between the larynx and the frontal wall of the abdomen, near the abdominal wall and stomach. The camera entrance is positioned away from these incisions to avoid complications.
The surgeon uses a surgical device to remove adhesions around the genital organs, ensuring clear visibility of the area. The device effectively prevents bleeding during the procedure.
The surgeon inspects the right fallopian tube, noting its normal appearance, while observing the surrounding pubic region.

The surgeon identifies a load in the Faleb Al-Yusra channel, extending from its beginning to end, indicating it cannot be preserved. The location for the cut is determined, and the knife is positioned away from the artery feeding the white tissue. The surgeon proceeds parallel to the Faleb channel, removing the knife completely after the cut.

The surgeon washes the pelvic area to ensure there is no bleeding at the site of the surgical incision. A sample is placed inside a plastic bag using the left valve channel and is removed from the abdominal cavity.
